meta.goal: Complete checkout and pay for the cart

score_rationales.usability: Band 50-65: the commitment point offers three equal mechanism-named actions, so the path to paying is ambiguous.
score_rationales.cognitive_load: Band 50-65: users must mentally total the order and resolve a three-way button choice unaided.
score_rationales.visual_layout: Band 66-79: grouping and hierarchy read correctly; the only focal-point conflict is the CTA row.
score_rationales.accessibility: Band 20-49: the primary CTA fails contrast badly (1.78:1) and all four inputs are unlabeled.
score_rationales.content: Band 50: several mechanism labels at the decision point and a dangling asterisk.
score_rationales.trust_persuasion: Band 50: card-entry form with no trust or security signals.

one_big_thing: The commitment moment is undecidable: three identical, washed-out, mechanism-named buttons with no visible order total mean a first-time buyer cannot tell which action takes their money or how much it will take.

findings.element: all four checkout inputs, left column
findings.issue: All four payment inputs rely on placeholder text as their only label.
findings.why_it_matters: Labels vanish once users type, so field identity is lost mid-entry and after autofill, causing card-entry errors.
findings.recommendation: Add persistent visible labels above each input.

findings.issue: Button text contrast is 1.78:1, far below the 4.5:1 minimum.
findings.why_it_matters: The actions that complete the purchase are barely legible, so many users cannot read them at all.
findings.recommendation: Darken the button fill to reach at least 4.5:1 against white text.

findings.issue: Three identically styled primary buttons compete at the single commitment point.
findings.why_it_matters: Equal visual weight gives no cue which action pays, so users hesitate or click the wrong one.
findings.recommendation: Keep one primary payment button; demote the others to secondary styles.

findings.issue: The paying action is labeled 'Submit' instead of naming its outcome.
findings.why_it_matters: Users expecting a review step get charged unexpectedly, creating disputes and abandoned carts.
findings.recommendation: Rename to 'Pay $242.00' once the total is computed.

findings.issue: The order summary lists item prices but no total.
findings.why_it_matters: Users must mentally sum three prices to decide, slowing commitment and inviting sticker shock later.
findings.recommendation: Add a computed total row directly above the payment action.

findings.element: Remove links under each order item
findings.issue: Remove links render at 1.85:1 contrast and roughly 8px text height.
findings.why_it_matters: Users who want to drop an item cannot find the control, so they abandon instead of editing the cart.
findings.recommendation: Restyle remove links to at least 4.5:1 contrast and 13px.

findings.issue: The shipping note ends in an asterisk that references no footnote.
findings.why_it_matters: An unexplained asterisk on cost information reads as hidden fees.
findings.recommendation: Remove the asterisk or state the shipping terms inline.

findings.issue: A high-attention promo banner competes with the payment task.
findings.why_it_matters: Mid-checkout promotion pulls focus from completing payment toward a detour.
findings.recommendation: Replace the banner with a plain promo-code field.

findings.issue: Card details are requested with no security cue or policy link anywhere.
findings.why_it_matters: First-time buyers at an unknown shop need credibility signals before typing a card number.
findings.recommendation: Add a payment-security indicator and policy links near the card fields.

clean_checks: GST-01/GST-04 — form fields and summary rows aligned and grouped to match semantics
GST-07 — order items correctly contained in the summary card boundary
JAKOB-01 — form-left / summary-right checkout layout follows convention
NLS-02 — item names and prices use plain user language
NLS-06 — cart contents stay visible beside the form; nothing to recall
NRM-02 — inputs and buttons are recognizably interactive; no false affordances (RF-01)
GP-06 — payment fields grouped in conventional order (card, expiry, CVC)
WCAG-2.5.8 — primary buttons ~50px tall, above the 24px desktop minimum
MILL-01 — three line items, well within working-memory limits
DARK-01..06 — no fake urgency, pre-checked costs, or confirmshaming (the dangling asterisk is filed as CNT-06)
